metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC2 — e.g. `grep -q 'key_not_valid_at_era' src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json && python -c "import json,sys; s=json.load(open('src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json')); assert 'key_not_valid_at_era' in s.get('description',''), 'grouping note missing'"` — or extend the held-out test to assert the description field contains the grouping text.
findings.finding: AC2 ('The schema description documents the verified/unverified/unsigned display grouping') has no concrete proving command — no grep, no schema-validation test, and no existing test is extended to assert the description field content. The executor could add the enum value and call it done without the description update being verified.
findings.suggested_fix: In `tests/unit/test_identity_ts3_117b_heldout.py`, add an assertion that the schema's top-level `description` field contains the string `'key_not_valid_at_era'` (or the full grouping phrase). Reference this test in AC2 as the proving command.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile 'INCLUDES key_not_valid_at_era' (What section) with 'asserts the full enum set' (AC1): pick one — exact equality against all five values is stronger and satisfies AC1; update both sections to use the same language.
findings.finding: The 'What' section instructs the implementer to update the held-out test so it asserts the enum 'INCLUDES key_not_valid_at_era' (a subset/membership check), while AC1 instructs the test to assert 'the full enum set' (an exact-equality check). These are different assertions and would send the implementer in two directions.
findings.location: What section (test update bullet) vs AC1
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, change to: 'a test asserts the enum equals exactly {"unsigned", "foreign_key", "certified", "mismatch", "key_not_valid_at_era"}'. In the What section, change 'INCLUDES' to 'equals exactly'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the test-update spec: either change 'What' to say 'assert the full enum set equals `{"unsigned", "foreign_key", "certified", "mismatch", "key_not_valid_at_era"}`' to match AC1, or explicitly call out that only a membership check is intended and remove 'full enum set' from AC1.
findings.finding: The 'What' section specifies updating the held-out test to assert the enum 'INCLUDES key_not_valid_at_era' (a membership/superset check), while AC1 specifies 'a test asserts the full enum set' (an equality/exact-set check). These are structurally different assertions — a membership check passes even if the enum accumulates extra undeclared values, while the equality check guards against drift. An executor cannot know which to implement, and the distinction is load-bearing for ongoing schema governance.
findings.location: What section ('assert the enum now INCLUDES key_not_valid_at_era') vs. AC1 ('a test asserts the full enum set')
findings.suggested_fix: In both 'What' and AC1, specify: 'assert `set(enum) == {"unsigned", "foreign_key", "certified", "mismatch", "key_not_valid_at_era"}`' — mirroring the existing equality pattern already used in the test at line 247.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either rename `test_verify_signature_schema_unchanged` (e.g., to `test_verify_signature_schema_verdict_enum`) or add an inline comment clarifying that the test now asserts the *updated* 5-value enum contract, so the name no longer contradicts the assertion.
findings.finding: The test being updated (`test_verify_signature_schema_unchanged`) retains a name that directly contradicts its new post-update assertion — the test will now assert the schema HAS changed, making the name semantically misleading and a future-maintainer trap. The plan doesn't address renaming or annotating the test to reflect its corrected intent.
findings.location: ## What — bullet 2: 'Update the TS3 held-out test `test_verify_signature_schema_unchanged`'
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to `test_verify_signature_schema_verdict_enum_complete` and update the docstring to: 'Asserts the full verdict enum as of epic AC10: [unsigned, foreign_key, certified, mismatch, key_not_valid_at_era].'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (and document in the plan) that no other tests, generated artifacts, or validators exhaustively enumerate the old 4-value verdict enum — or enumerate and address any that do.
findings.finding: The plan enumerates only two consumers of the schema enum (the held-out test and the drift gates), but does not confirm whether other exhaustive enum consumers exist — such as generated type stubs, other unit/integration tests that assert the old 4-value set, or validators that will silently accept or reject the new value. If any such consumers exist, they are not acknowledged.
findings.location: ## What — bullet 3: 'Regenerate/keep green any schema-derived drift gates (public-types / output-schema)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/types.py` (regenerated via `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types`) to `file_impact`, since its `VerifySignatureResult.verdict` Literal is derived from the schema enum and must be updated alongside the schema change.
findings.finding: src/rebar/types.py contains a schema-derived Literal for the verdict field (line 237: `verdict: Literal["unsigned", "foreign_key", "certified", "mismatch"]`) generated by gen_types.py from the schema enum. The plan acknowledges drift-gate refresh in prose and AC3 but omits src/rebar/types.py from the file_impact list. An implementer working only from file_impact will miss regenerating this file, causing the public-types CI drift gate (test_public_types.py / gen_types) to fail.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/types.py` to the `file_impact` section with note: `(regenerated — VerifySignatureResult.verdict Literal gains 'key_not_valid_at_era')`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/types.py` (regenerated via `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types`) to `file_impact`, since the schema enum change will update the `VerifySignatureResult.verdict` `Literal` type in this generated file.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/types.py` is a schema-derived file auto-generated from `verify_signature_result.schema.json` by `gen_types.py`. Adding `key_not_valid_at_era` to the schema enum will change the generated `Literal[...]` type for `VerifySignatureResult.verdict` in `types.py` (currently `Literal["unsigned", "foreign_key", "certified", "mismatch"]` at line 237). The plan's `file_impact` section omits `src/rebar/types.py` as an edit target, even though the drift gate (`tests/interfaces/contracts/test_public_types.py`) will fail unless this file is regenerated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an entry to `file_impact`: `src/rebar/types.py` (regenerated via `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types` — the verdict `Literal` will gain `'key_not_valid_at_era'`)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/types.py` to `file_impact` (regenerated by `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types`) so the implementer explicitly runs the generator and commits the updated `VerifySignatureResult.verdict` Literal.
findings.finding: src/rebar/types.py — a generated file whose VerifySignatureResult.verdict Literal will change from 4 to 5 values when the schema is edited — is absent from file_impact, creating a risk that the implementer skips explicitly regenerating and committing it, causing the CI drift-gate to fail.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to file_impact: `src/rebar/types.py` (regenerated — verdict Literal gains `key_not_valid_at_era`; run `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types` after the schema edit).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename `test_verify_signature_schema_unchanged` to `test_verify_signature_schema_includes_key_not_valid_at_era` (or similar) and update the held-out file's line-9 docstring to reflect that the schema now includes the new verdict.
findings.finding: The plan updates `test_verify_signature_schema_unchanged` to assert the schema HAS been changed (enum now includes the new value), but does not rename the test. After the update the function name directly contradicts its assertion — a pink-elephant antipattern where the name embeds an active false claim ('unchanged') that will mislead future readers, reviewers, and CI failure messages.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to `test_verify_signature_verdict_enum_includes_all_five_values` and update the docstring bullet at line 9 to: '`verify_signature_result.schema.json` verdict enum includes `key_not_valid_at_era` (epic AC10)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/types.py` to `file_impact` with the note '(regenerated by `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types`; verdict Literal gains `key_not_valid_at_era`)'.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/types.py` contains a schema-derived `Literal["unsigned", "foreign_key", "certified", "mismatch"]` at line 237 (generated by `gen_types.py` from `verify_signature_result.schema.json`). The plan's Scope says to 'Regenerate/keep green any schema-derived drift gates (public-types / output-schema)' and AC3 names the public-types drift gate, but `src/rebar/types.py` is absent from `file_impact`. An executor using `file_impact` as their implementation checklist will not regenerate this file, causing the Public-types drift gate (`gen_types --check`) to fail on the stale `Literal`.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to file_impact: `- src/rebar/types.py` (verdict Literal regenerated via `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types`; gains `"key_not_valid_at_era"`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exact commands to run each named drift gate in AC3 (e.g. `make public-types-check` and `pytest tests/drift/test_output_schema_coverage.py`) so the executor can mechanically prove the criterion without repo archaeology.
findings.finding: AC3 names drift gates ('public-types', 'output-schema coverage') but provides no proving commands — no `make` target, no script path, no `pytest -k` filter — so an executor cannot mechanically verify the gates are green without discovering the commands independently.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 / What section ('Regenerate/keep green any schema-derived drift gates')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Verify with `<exact-gate-command-1>` and `<exact-gate-command-2>` (or equivalent CI job names) both exit 0 after the schema change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out step (e.g., 'revert schema + revert test + re-run drift gates') and document whether any drift-gate or downstream consumer performs exact-equality validation on the verdict enum, confirming the additive change is safe for all consumers.
findings.finding: The plan changes a schema enum that had an explicit held-out test guarding its exact shape, and acknowledges the test must be updated, but provides no rollback/back-out plan and no audit of other strict consumers (validators, exhaustive switch statements, output-schema drift gates) that may treat the 4-value enum as a fixed contract. T4 requires an explicit rollback/reversibility mechanism, not just acknowledgment of the change.
findings.location: What section (test update bullet); Scope section; AC3

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
